raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California's current law fails to recognize the significant impact threats or injuries to beloved pets have on stalking victims.",
    "Neglecting to update California's anti-stalking law leaves victims and their innocent pets vulnerable to stalking threats and attacks.",
    "Stalking poses a significant threat to the emotional and physical well-being of victims, including their beloved pets.",
    "Stalkers often target pets as a means to intimidate, harass, or send threatening messages to their victims.",
    "An estimated one in three women and one in six men in the United States report enduring stalking at some point in their lives.",
    "One in 15 women and one in 24 men in the United States report being stalked in the last 12 months.",
    "Stalking behaviors can be committed in person, by following the victim, or by monitoring and harassing the victim electronically.",
    "Stalking is a crime of power and control that causes victims to fear for their safety or the safety of their loved ones.",
    "Perpetrators of stalking tend to damage their victim’s property including pets in order to manipulate them.",
    "Four in 10 stalkers threaten a victim or the victim’s family, friends, co-workers, or family pet.",
    "87,020 threats to harm a pet have been reported according to the National Crime Victimization Survey.",
    "SB 89 would make it a crime to engage in conduct with the intent to harm, intimidate, or place under surveillance another person, their family members, or their pets.",
    "California’s law ignores how powerful a threat or injury to a beloved pet can be."
  ]
}
```
